headline: Survey at 29% completion; strong regional coverage but scaling readiness lags partnership satisfaction.
collection_status: 393 of 1,350 responses collected (29% progress). All 10 target regions active with Lindi, Mara, and Tanga leading (186 responses combined). Collection pace must accelerate 4.4x to meet target by survey close.
key_findings: KiuFunza expansion dominates priorities (139/393, 35%), followed by new programs (92) and policy integration (83), signaling strong demand for scale but weak institutional readiness (2.7/5).
Teacher respondents comprise 84% of sample (153 lower + 54 upper KF + 4 non-KF), but district/regional officials severely underrepresented (15 of 393, 4%)—risking policy-level insight gaps.

Partner satisfaction high (4.4/5) yet scale readiness critically low (2.7/5)—suggests stakeholders trust LearnImpact but doubt systems/capacity for expansion; feedback emphasizes need for teacher incentives, digital monitoring, and government coordination.
Rukwa region lags significantly (9 responses vs. regional average 39)—requires urgent targeted mobilization or risks skewing national-level analysis.
flags: Scale readiness (2.7/5) vs. expansion demand mismatch: 139 respondents want KF expansion but survey data doesn't reflect readiness assessment granularity; immediate gap analysis needed.
District and regional official undersampling (4%): ARCs, REOs, DEOs, and RCs severely underrepresented; policy translation risk if leadership voice remains weak in final dataset.
Rukwa region critical lag: 9 responses vs. 70 in Lindi; without urgent mobilization, regional representation will be skewed and sub-national insights compromised.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted mobilization to Rukwa and undersampled regions (Singida, Songwe) plus dedicated outreach to district/regional officials (DEOs, ARCs, REOs) to secure 60+ additional responses by next collection cycle. Simultaneously, conduct rapid scale-readiness diagnostic with top 50 respondents to translate demand into implementation barriers.
